InfraDiscovery.keywords: infra-discovery, infrastructure, tooling, dev-environment, tool-stack, developer-workflow, rule-activation, modes, vcs, git, sdd-infra-track

Belief_State.Axiom:
Infrastructure track is orthogonal to application track. This directive runs in its own session, produces its own spec, never reads application specs. Conversely, `discovery` and `module-decomposition` never read this spec — they own application concerns; this owns tooling.
Tracks converge ONLY at `task-scaffolding`, which consumes both spec trees if both exist (or only one for single-track projects).

**Three modes, auto-detected from intake:** - `greenfield` — no infra spec exists; default when no spec link. - `extension` — add tools / categories to existing infra spec without replacing decisions. - `pivot` — replace tool choices (swap pnpm → bun, replace test framework, change bundler). Triggers supersession in Decision Log + downstream invalidation list.

Mode is mechanical; ambiguity halts. Detection considers both intake verb and presence of `specs/README.md` (Specs Portal — see `AX_SPECS_PORTAL_COOWNERSHIP`).
| Portal exists? | Intake spec link? | Verb | Mode | |---|---|---|---| | No | No | — | `greenfield` | | No | Yes | extend / add tool / add category / расширь / добавь | `extension` | | No | Yes | pivot / replace / swap / заменим / поменяем | `pivot` | | No | Yes | none of the above | HALT, ask binary: extension or pivot | | Yes | any | extend / add tool / add category / расширь / добавь | `extension` | | Yes | any | pivot / replace / swap / заменим / поменяем | `pivot` | | Yes | any | none of the above | HALT (`H_PORTAL_PRESENT_NO_VERB`), ask binary: extension or pivot. Greenfield is FORBIDDEN when Portal exists. |
Spec link or Portal is treated as authoritative, not inspiration.

`specs/README.md` is the Specs Portal — single entry point for any future session — co-owned by `discovery`, `infra-discovery`, `module-decomposition`. Each directive owns specific rows / sections; reads other rows for context; writes only its own.
**`infra-discovery` owns:** - `## Knowledge Graph` row pointing to `_infrastructure/_infrastructure.spec.md` with one-line tool stack summary (e.g., «TS + pnpm + vitest + biome + lefthook»).
**`infra-discovery` reads (does not edit):** - `## Vision` section (owned by `discovery`); - `## Knowledge Graph` row pointing to root spec (owned by `discovery`); - `## Knowledge Graph` rows pointing to module specs (owned by `module-decomposition`).
Portal lifecycle in `infra-discovery`: - **`greenfield`** — Portal does NOT exist; `infra-discovery` CREATES it per `SPECS_PORTAL_STRUCTURE` with own row + placeholder lines for absent application track. `## Vision` is OMITTED entirely (SANDBOX-style project until `discovery` runs and adds it). - **`greenfield` + Portal exists from another track** — paradox: greenfield mode says Portal absent, but Portal exists. This is a contradiction; HALT (`H_PORTAL_PRESENT_NO_VERB`). - **`extension`** — Portal exists; UPDATE own row's tool-stack summary; never touch foreign rows. - **`pivot`** — Portal exists; REWRITE own row's tool-stack summary to reflect new stack; never touch foreign rows.
Editing rows owned by other directives is forbidden (`H_PORTAL_FOREIGN_ROW_EDIT`).

**Phase reads specs only, never code, never project-wide search (grep / find / ripgrep).** Allowed inputs by mode: - `greenfield` — operator input + tool documentation passed by operator (links / supplied content) + rule files at `.ai/directives//.xml`. Local `specs/_infrastructure/` is NOT scanned even if present (but `specs/README.md` existence is detected by stat for mode-detection per `AX_MODE_AUTO_DETECT_OR_HALT`; if it exists, mode is NOT greenfield). - `extension` / `pivot` — same + `specs/README.md` Portal (read in full for context) + the linked existing infra spec.
Spec/code mismatch is resolved by `audit` (only directive that compares spec against code).

Tool categories are declared explicitly before tool choices. Two groups:
**Mandatory** (every project has them): - `vcs` — version control policy (gitignore baseline, branch strategy, commit convention, LFS). Resolves to `.ai/directives/vcs/.xml` (default: `git.xml`). - `package-management` — npm / pnpm / bun / yarn / cargo / go-modules / poetry / uv / … - `git-hooks` — lefthook / husky / simple-git-hooks / pre-commit / … (automation runner; works with `vcs` but distinct: tool vs policy).
**Optional** (subset selected based on operator intent): - `type-check` — tsc / tsgo / mypy / pyright / … (typed languages only) - `linting` — biome / eslint / oxlint / golangci-lint / ruff / clippy / … - `formatting` — often combined with linter (biome / prettier / gofmt / black / …) - `test-unit` — vitest / jest / node:test / pytest / go-test / cargo-test / … - `test-e2e` — playwright / cypress / selenium / … (UI only) - `test-ui` — storybook / histoire / ladle / … (UI components only) - `bundler` — vite / esbuild / rollup / webpack / rspack / … (browser/SPA target only) - `ci` — github-actions / gitlab-ci / circleci / jenkins / … - `monorepo-tool` — turbo / nx / lerna / nrwl / … (multi-package repos only) - `docs` — typedoc / mkdocs / vitepress / …
Agent proposes a relevant subset based on intent (e.g., SANDBOX without UI doesn't need bundler / e2e / UI test). Operator approves the category list before tool choices begin. Mandatory categories CANNOT be removed.

**Two stages: pick rules, then walk through axioms. Never activate silently.**
- **Stage A — Relevance per category.** For each category with rules available: shortlist «recommend activate» + «considered, recommend skip», each with one-line rationale scoped to project context. For mandatory rules (per `AX_VCS_CATEGORY_MANDATORY` etc.): still emit rationale («mandatory by axiom + applies because »). Operator: agree / expand / contract → list locked. Deviations (especially overrides of mandatory rules) → Decision Log.
Stage A example (SANDBOX, `quality` category): ``` Available: eslint-rules.xml Recommend skipping: eslint-rules.xml — opinionated «binary severity, no warn» policy is overkill for throwaway sandbox scripts; ESLint itself can be configured permissively without this meta-policy. Operator: agree / expand / contract? ``` - **Stage B — Per-rule axiom walk-through (rules with `activation=now` only).** Read only `Mission`, `Belief_State`, `Reward_Criteria` of the rule file (skip `Definitions` / `Code_Patterns` / `Anti_Patterns` / `Verification_Hooks`). Surface each axiom with rationale → operator Accept (default) / Adapt (Decision Log) / Override (Decision Log). No badgering on uncontroversial axioms.
**Activation defaults.** `vcs/*`, `runtimes/*`, `quality/*`, `architecture/*` → `now`. `coding/*`, `testing/*` → `later`. Stage A still applies to `later` rules (brief inclusion rationale); Stage B walk-through deferred to `task-execution`.
All activation outcomes recorded in §6 Decision Log of the infra spec.

Chosen tools must compose. After all categories decided, run cross-check: - Linter conflicts with formatter? (e.g., biome auto-covers formatting → no separate formatter needed.) - Test runner integrates with type-check? (e.g., vitest + tsc.) - Package manager works in monorepo mode if requested? - CI provider supports all chosen tool versions?
Conflicts surface as findings → operator resolves by re-picking → `H_TOOL_INTERACTION_CONFLICT` if unresolvable.

Spec includes a Developer Workflow Example before final approval. Like Discovery's Golden DX, but in shell-script form. References ONLY chosen tools with resolved commands; no placeholders. Per `DEVELOPER_WORKFLOW_EXAMPLE_FORMAT`.

Tool choices imply rule files; the spec lists effective rules. TypeScript chosen → `.ai/directives/coding/typescript-rules.xml`. vitest → `.ai/directives/testing/vitest.xml`. biome → `.ai/directives/quality/biome.xml`. Etc.
Rule files must already exist in `.ai/directives/`. Tool chosen with no rule file → `H_TOOL_RULE_MISSING` (halt).
Spec emits an Effective Rules table that becomes input for `task-scaffolding`'s Cascade Table (source: `infra`).

Infrastructure file structure lives at repo root. Configs like `package.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `biome.json`, `vitest.config.ts`, `lefthook.yml`, `.github/workflows/*.yml` sit at root, not under `src/`. Spec maps each chosen tool to its config file path.

Every tool choice is a Decision Log entry. Mandatory triggers: choice among 2+ candidates per category; knowingly accepted risk (e.g., picking bun despite young ecosystem); departure from default recommendation; change/cancellation of a prior decision.
Format per `DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT`. Decisions without rationale are not recorded.

In `pivot` mode each replaced tool requires explicit supersession. - Read existing infra spec; identify D-NNN entries being changed. - Each new D-NNN that replaces an old one carries `Supersedes: D-XXX`. - Old D-XXX entry: `Status` flips active → superseded; body content stays. - Spec body (Tool Stack table, File Structure, Workflow Example) is **reworked in place** — old content REMOVED, preserved by git history + self-contained rework D-NNN entry (per `AX_REWORK_DECISION_LOG_SELF_CONTAINED`).

Pivot rework D-NNN entries are self-contained for an agent loading the current spec without git context. Format per `REWORK_DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT`. The Decision Log entry alone reconstructs context for downstream phases.
Compact header on every message. Global: `ID_R001`, `ID_R002`, … ASCII uppercase + digits + `_` only.

- **Phase agreement.** Agent never closes a phase autonomously. Signal `STATUS=READY_TO_ADVANCE`; operator confirms. Phase Progress every meaningful round. - **Neutral + critical.** No auto-agree («давай pnpm») without rationale. Surface trade-offs (bun's speed vs npm's ubiquity); request justification; record only with explicit operator rationale. Don't push a single viewpoint either. - **Confidence explicit** (LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH). Under LOW: do not lock the tool stack; do not move to file structure. - **Context budget.** Long dialogues: top-3 questions; Phase Progress as delta; no full re-listing of unchanged categories.
All tool choices, rationale, file structure, effective rules baked into the spec markdown. `task-scaffolding` reads only the final infra spec, not this conversation.

Operator-facing prose in **Russian**; code and canonical identifiers in **English**. - Russian: spec body text (Vision, Tool choice rationale, Decision Log `Why`/`Risk`, Workflow Example comments), README files, halt messages emitted to operator, Phase Progress field content. - English: code, JSDoc, file paths, tool commands, axiom IDs (`AX_*`), Trace anchors, IDs (`D-NNN`/`H_*`), severity/status tokens, structural section headings, field labels (`**Status:**`, `**Why:**`).

§7 Handoff lists: which infra spec is primary input; setup tasks to scaffold (tool installation + config + smoke test); effective rules table for cascade; whether application track is expected to coexist or this is an infra-only project (SANDBOX-style).

InfraDiscovery.Halt_Conditions: | 🛑 ID | Trigger | |---|---| | `H_NO_INTENT` | Operator passed empty invocation | | `H_AMBIGUOUS_MODE` | Spec link present + verb absent → ask extension or pivot | | `H_PORTAL_PRESENT_NO_VERB` | `specs/README.md` exists but intake has no extension/pivot verb → ask binary; greenfield forbidden | | `H_PORTAL_FOREIGN_ROW_EDIT` | Attempted to edit a Portal row owned by `discovery` or `module-decomposition` | | `H_LOW_CONFIDENCE` | Confidence LOW at tool stack lock or final spec | | `H_CATEGORIES_NOT_APPROVED` | Attempted STEP_3 tool choices without category list approved | | `H_VCS_RULE_MISSING` | `.ai/directives/vcs/git.xml` absent | | `H_TOOL_RULE_MISSING` | Tool chosen but its rule file does not resolve to an existing `.ai/directives//.xml` | | `H_TOOL_INTERACTION_CONFLICT` | Cross-check surfaces unresolvable conflict between chosen tools | | `H_OPERATOR_REJECT` | Operator refuses categories / tool choice / Decision Log entry / workflow / file structure / final spec |
InfraDiscovery.Golden_Example:
Reference compressed infra spec for a polyglot SANDBOX project (TypeScript + Go + Python). Demonstrates Tool Stack table, Developer Workflow Example, File Structure, Effective Rules block.
```markdown # sandbox: Infrastructure Specification (InfraDiscovery)
## 1. Vision Polyglot personal sandbox для коротких экспериментов. Разработчик создаёт скрипт одной командой, пишет код, запускает, смотрит вывод. Поддержка TS, Go, Python из коробки. Production deployment вне scope.
## 2. Tool Stack
### 2.1 Categories Covered package-management, type-check (TS), linting+formatting, test-unit, git-hooks. Bundler/e2e/UI/CI исключены — sandbox local-only.
### 2.2 Tool Choices
| Category | Tool | Rationale | |---|---|---| | package-management | pnpm | D-001 — workspace для multi-language structure | | type-check | tsc (strict) | D-002 — стандарт, без exotic | | linting+formatting | biome | D-003 — single tool для TS/JSON | | linting (Go) | golangci-lint | D-004 — стандарт | | linting (Py) | ruff | D-005 — speed + единый tool | | test-unit (TS) | vitest | D-006 — fast, ESM-native | | test-unit (Go) | go test | D-007 — встроенный | | test-unit (Py) | pytest | D-008 — стандарт | | git-hooks | lefthook | D-009 — Go binary, без node-deps в hooks |
## 3. Developer Workflow Example
```bash # Setup — once after clone pnpm install go mod download uv sync pnpm exec lefthook install
# New TypeScript experiment pnpm new-script my-ts-experiment ts pnpm tsx scripts/my-ts-experiment.ts
# Test all pnpm test # vitest go test ./... uv run pytest
# Lint all pnpm lint # biome golangci-lint run uv run ruff check .
# Pre-commit hook fires automatically: lint + format + tests for changed files ```
## 4. File Structure
``` sandbox/ ├── package.json ├── pnpm-workspace.yaml ├── biome.json ├── tsconfig.json ├── vitest.config.ts ├── lefthook.yml ├── go.mod ├── .golangci.yml ├── pyproject.toml └── scripts/ ```
## 5. Effective Rules (for cascade)
| Rule | Category | Source | |---|---|---| | typescript-rules | coding | infra (D-002) | | go-rules | coding | infra (D-007) | | python-rules | coding | infra (D-008) | | biome | quality | infra (D-003) | | golangci-lint | quality | infra (D-004) | | ruff | quality | infra (D-005) | | vitest | testing | infra (D-006) | | pytest | testing | infra (D-008) |
## 6. Decision Log [D-001 … D-009 per `DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT`.]
## 7. Handoff to task-scaffolding - **Primary input:** `specs/_infrastructure/_infrastructure.spec.md`. - **Application track present?** No — infrastructure-only project (SANDBOX-style). Tasks scaffolded ONLY for setup. - **Setup tasks to scaffold:** bootstrap pnpm workspace + tsconfig; configure biome; configure vitest; configure Go modules; configure uv + pyproject; configure lefthook hooks; implement `pnpm new-script` generator; README onboarding. - **Effective rules ready for cascade:** see §5. - **Open risks:** Go + TS + Python в одном hooks runner — нужна проверка lefthook stages для всех трёх. ```

Step.Goal: Detect Portal presence + mode (`AX_MODE_AUTO_DETECT_OR_HALT`); open `INTENT_CAPTURE` frame.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__INTAKE`). 2. Empty intake → `H_NO_INTENT`. Ask operator for tooling intent or spec link. 3. **Portal detection.** Stat `specs/README.md`: - exists → read it in full (Vision + Knowledge Graph). NOT greenfield. Apply Portal-aware rows of mode detection table; verb absent → `H_PORTAL_PRESENT_NO_VERB`. - absent → greenfield candidate. Apply Portal-absent rows. 4. Apply mode detection table per `AX_MODE_AUTO_DETECT_OR_HALT`. 5. `extension` / `pivot`: read the linked infra spec (or infra spec referenced from Portal Knowledge Graph). `pivot`: capture `pre-rework git ref` from operator (or current HEAD before changes). 6. Open frame `INTENT_CAPTURE`. Proceed to STEP_1.

Each round: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__INTENT_CAPTURE`). 2. Intent Candidate: 1–3 bullets describing what the infra enables (multi-language? monorepo? production-grade vs experimental? UI / SPA / backend / CLI?). 3. Working State: Confirmed / Open / Risks. 4. Questions (top-3); ≥1 «uncomfortable» about constraints (peak builds? CI minutes? team size? offline dev?). 5. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.
Operator: confirm intent freeze → STEP_2; clarify → repeat STEP_1.

Step.Goal: Agree on the list of categories to cover (`AX_TOOL_CATEGORIES_EXPLICIT`).
Step.Action:
1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__TOOL_CATEGORIES`). 2. Propose subset based on intent. Justify exclusions briefly («no bundler — no browser target»; «no e2e — no UI»). 3. Categories Draft: numbered list with one-line purpose each. Mandatory categories per `AX_TOOL_CATEGORIES_EXPLICIT` always present. 4. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.
Without approved categories, transition to STEP_3 forbidden → `H_CATEGORIES_NOT_APPROVED`.

Step.Goal: For each category, pick a tool via Design Variants + Decision Log entry.
Step.Action:
Iterate over categories: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__TOOL_CHOICES__`). 2. Variant Set per `AX_TOOL_DESIGN_VARIANTS_PER_CATEGORY`: 2–3 candidate tools; idea (1 sentence per tool); trade-offs (speed, ecosystem maturity, learning curve, lock-in, monorepo support); recommended default with explicit recommendation flag. 3. Critical Analysis: which variant carries more risk and why. 4. Operator picks → Mandatory Rationale Pull («Why this tool? What's critical, what's consciously sacrificed?»). 5. Decision Log entry per `DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT` (D-NNN). 6. Effective Rule Resolution: name the rule file `.ai/directives//.xml` implied by the choice. File missing → `H_TOOL_RULE_MISSING`. 7. Phase Progress + Approval Check (per category). STOP.
After all categories: run **Tool Interaction Check** per `AX_TOOL_INTERACTION_CHECK`. Resolve conflicts via re-pick. Unresolvable → `H_TOOL_INTERACTION_CONFLICT`.

1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__DEVELOPER_WORKFLOW`). 2. Workflow Example Draft per `DEVELOPER_WORKFLOW_EXAMPLE_FORMAT`: shell script with intent comments — setup, daily flow, debug flow. References ONLY chosen tools with resolved commands. 3. DX Reading Notes: visible dev expectations. 4. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.

1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__FILE_STRUCTURE`). 2. Tree Draft: ASCII tree of repo root with config files mapped to chosen tools. 3. File Mapping: each config file → which tool / category it serves. 4. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.

Step.Goal: Assemble the infra spec + write/update Specs Portal.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__FINAL_SPEC_DRAFT`). 2. Generate `specs/_infrastructure/_infrastructure.spec.md` per `INFRA_SPEC_MARKDOWN_STRUCTURE`. In `pivot` mode also generate §7.1 per `PIVOT_INVALIDATION_LIST_FORMAT` and rework D-NNN entries per `REWORK_DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT`. 3. **Specs Portal write/update per `AX_SPECS_PORTAL_COOWNERSHIP` + `SPECS_PORTAL_STRUCTURE`:** - `greenfield`: CREATE `specs/README.md` WITHOUT `## Vision` (SANDBOX-style infra-only project until application track launches) + `## Knowledge Graph` containing only the `_infrastructure/` row + placeholder line for absent application track. - `extension`: read existing Portal; UPDATE own `_infrastructure/` row's tool-stack summary; leave foreign rows untouched. - `pivot`: read existing Portal; REWRITE own row's tool-stack summary to reflect new stack; leave foreign rows untouched. 4. Output both artifacts for review. STOP.

Frame_Rules.Rule: Push frame on: tool trade-off challenge requiring deeper analysis; alternative tool not in variant set; specific monorepo / CI / hooks integration concern.
Pop on confirmed local conclusion, DEFERRED status, or topic doesn't block current category.

Output_Contracts.Contract: ```text 🏗️ ID_R | STACK: ROOT__ | MODE: | <📂|🚧|↩️|⏭️|💤> | CONF: 🎯 GOAL: ⏭️ EXIT: ```
```markdown 🎯 Phase Progress — ✅ Closed: ⏳ Open: 🛑 Blocking: ▶️ Next: 📊 Counters: ```

```markdown ### D-NNN — : - **Status:** active | superseded | rejected | deferred - **Recorded:** session InfraDiscovery, - **Why:** <operator's own wording> - **Risk accepted:** - **Rejected alternatives:** - **Implies rule file:** `.ai/directives//.xml` - **Supersedes:** D-NNN (in pivot) ```

In `pivot` mode (per `AX_REWORK_DECISION_LOG_SELF_CONTAINED`):
```markdown ### D-NNN — : → - **Status:** active - **Recorded:** session InfraDiscovery (pivot mode), - **Was → Now:** - **Why:** <operator's own wording> - **Risk accepted:** - **Supersedes:** D-XXX - **Pre-rework state:** git ref `` ```
Self-contained for an agent loading the current spec without git context.

Shell-script-style scenario with intent comments, covering: - Setup (clone, install) - Daily flow (create file, run, test, lint) - Commit flow (hooks behavior) - Debug flow (how to inspect / reproduce / fix when something breaks)
References ONLY chosen tools with resolved commands. No placeholders.

Augmenting §7 Handoff in `pivot` mode:
```markdown ### 7.1 Pivot Invalidation List - **Tasks requiring reopen:** - TSK-NN — - **Rule files to update or remove:** - `.ai/directives/.xml` — superseded by D-NNN - **Application root spec rules to revisit (if app track exists):** - ```
Empty subsections must be explicitly `None`, not omitted.

`specs/README.md` — Specs Portal, co-owned per `AX_SPECS_PORTAL_COOWNERSHIP`. Flat list under `## Knowledge Graph`; each row owned by exactly one directive. Empty placeholder lines for absent tracks (so future sessions know what to launch).
```markdown # — Specs Portal
## Vision
## Knowledge Graph - [`.spec.md`](./.spec.md) — root spec проекта. - [`_infrastructure/`](./_infrastructure/_infrastructure.spec.md) — . - [`/`](.//.spec.md) — . ```
Greenfield-create skeleton when `infra-discovery` runs first (SANDBOX-style; only the writing directive's row is real, others are placeholder lines): ```markdown # — Specs Portal
## Knowledge Graph - [`_infrastructure/`](./_infrastructure/_infrastructure.spec.md) — . - _Application: не описана — запусти `discovery` если проект перестаёт быть infra-only._ - _Modules: не выделены — запусти `module-decomposition` после `discovery`._ ```

Final `specs/_infrastructure/_infrastructure.spec.md` is 100% self-contained for stateless handoff.
```markdown # : Infrastructure Specification (InfraDiscovery)
## 1. Vision [What dev/ops experience the infra enables. Nothing about application goals.]
## 2. Tool Stack
### 2.1 Categories Covered [List of categories + brief justification of excluded ones.]
### 2.2 Tool Choices | Category | Tool | Rationale link | |---|---|---| | | | D-NNN |
## 3. Developer Workflow Example [Per `DEVELOPER_WORKFLOW_EXAMPLE_FORMAT`.]
## 4. File Structure [ASCII tree of repo root + File Mapping.]
## 5. Effective Rules (for cascade) | Rule | Category | Source | |---|---|---| | | coding \| testing \| architecture \| quality | infra (D-NNN) |
## 6. Decision Log [D-NNN entries per `DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT`. Pivot rework entries per `REWORK_DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT`.]
## 7. Handoff to task-scaffolding - **Primary input for `task-scaffolding`:** `specs/_infrastructure/_infrastructure.spec.md` (this file). - **Application track present?** Yes (specs/.spec.md exists) | No (infra-only project). - **Setup tasks to scaffold:** [list — bootstrap, configure, smoke-test per tool] - **Effective rules ready for cascade:** see §5. - **Open risks:** [not-closed items]
```
